title: Summation notation
description: Practice your understanding of sigma notation of finite sums.
topic_path: Riemann sums, summation notation, and definite integral notation

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the sum $2+5+8+11$.

**Which expression is equal to the above sum?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Let's evaluate the two sigma expressions and see which of them results in $2+5+8+11$.
In conclusion, both $\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^4(3n-1)$ and $\displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^3(2+3n)$ are equal to $2+5+8+11$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the sum $5 + (-7) + (-27) + (-55)$.

**Which expression is equal to the above sum?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Let's evaluate the two sigma expressions and see which of them results in $5 + (-7) + (-27) + (-55)$.
In conclusion, both $\displaystyle\sum_{k=1}^4\left(9-4k^2\right)$ and $\displaystyle\sum_{k=1}^4\left(2k+3)(3-2k\right)$ are equal to $5 + (-7) + (-27) + (-55)$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the sum $0 + 0.25 + 0.5 + 0.75$.

**Which expression is equal to the above sum?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Let's evaluate the two sigma expressions and see which of them results in $0 + 0.25 + 0.5 + 0.75$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\sum_{m=0}^3\left(\dfrac{m}{4}\right)
\\\\
&=\left[\dfrac{(0)}{4}\right]+\left[\dfrac{(1)}{4}\right]+\left[\dfrac{(2)}{4}\right]+\left[\dfrac{(3)}{4}\right]
\\\\
&=0 + 0.25 + 0.5 + 0.75
\\\\\\
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\sum_{m=1}^4\left(\dfrac{2m}{8}\right)
\\\\
&=\left[\dfrac{2(1)}{8}\right]+\left[\dfrac{2(2)}{8}\right]+\left[\dfrac{2(3)}{8}\right]+\left[\dfrac{2(4)}{8}\right]
\\\\
&=0.25 + 0.5 + 0.75 + 1
\end{align}$
In conclusion, only $\displaystyle\sum_{m=0}^3\left(\dfrac{m}{4}\right)$ is equal to $0 + 0.25 + 0.5 + 0.75$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the sum $(-3) + (-11) + (-19) + (-27)$.

**Which expression is equal to the above sum?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Let's evaluate the two sigma expressions and see which of them results in $(-3) + (-11) + (-19) + (-27)$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^4\left(5-8n\right)
\\\\
&=\left[5-8(1)\right]+\left[5-8(2)\right]+\left[5-8(3)\right]+\left[5-8(4)\right]
\\\\
&=-3 + (-11) + (-19) + (-27)
\\\\\\
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^3\left(6n-3\right)
\\\\
&=\left[6(0)-3\right]+\left[6(1)-3\right]+\left[6(2)-3\right]+\left[6(3)-3\right]
\\\\
&=(-3) + 3 + 9 + 15
\end{align}$
In conclusion, only $\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^4\left(5-8n\right)$ is equal to $(-3) + (-11) + (-19) + (-27)$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the sum $4 + 25 + 64 + 121$.

**Which expression is equal to the above sum?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Let's evaluate the two sigma expressions and see which of them results in $4 + 25 + 64 + 121$.
In conclusion, only $\displaystyle\sum_{i=0}^3(3i+2)^2$ is equal to $4 + 25 + 64 + 121$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the sum $-\dfrac{2}{5}  -\dfrac{2}{7}  -\dfrac{2}{9}  -\dfrac{2}{11}$.

**Which expression is equal to the above sum?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Let's evaluate the two sigma expressions and see which of them results in $-\dfrac{2}{5} -\dfrac{2}{7}  -\dfrac{2}{9}  -\dfrac{2}{11}$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\sum_{j=1}^4\left(-\dfrac{2}{4+j}\right)
\\\\
&=\left[-\dfrac{2}{4+(1)}\right]+\left[-\dfrac{2}{4+(2)}\right]+\left[-\dfrac{2}{4+(3)}\right]+\left[-\dfrac{2}{4+(4)}\right]
\\\\
&=-\dfrac{2}{5} -\dfrac{1}{3} -\dfrac{2}{7} -\dfrac{1}{4}
\\\\\\
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\sum_{j=1}^4\left(-\dfrac{2}{2j+3}\right)
\\\\
&=\left[-\dfrac{2}{2(1)+3}\right]+\left[-\dfrac{2}{2(2)+3}\right]+\left[-\dfrac{2}{2(3)+3}\right]+\left[-\dfrac{2}{2(4)+3}\right]
\\\\
&=-\dfrac{2}{5} -\dfrac{2}{7} -\dfrac{2}{9} -\dfrac{2}{11}
\end{align}$
In conclusion, only $\displaystyle\sum_{j=1}^4\left(-\dfrac{2}{2j+3}\right)$ is equal to $-\dfrac{2}{5} -\dfrac{2}{7} -\dfrac{2}{9} -\dfrac{2}{11}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider the sum $-9 + 0 + 9 + 18$.

**Which expression is equal to the above sum?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Let's evaluate the two sigma expressions and see which of them results in $-9 + 0 + 9 + 18$.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\sum_{k=0}^3\left(4k-9\right)
\\\\
&=\left[4(0)-9\right]+\left[4(1)-9\right]+\left[4(2)-9\right]+\left[4(3)-9\right]
\\\\
&=-9 -5 -1 + 3
\\\\\\
&\phantom{=}\displaystyle\sum_{k=1}^4\left(1-10k\right)
\\\\
&=\left[1-10(1)\right]+\left[1-10(2)\right]+\left[1-10(3)\right]+\left[1-10(4)\right]
\\\\
&=-9 -19 -29 -39
\end{align}$
In conclusion, neither sigma expression is equal to $-9 + 0 + 9 + 18$.

Consider the sum $4 + 5 + 8 + 13$.

**Which expression is equal to the above sum?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Let's evaluate the two sigma expressions and see which of them results in $4 + 5 + 8 + 13$.
In conclusion, neither sigma expression is equal to $4 + 5 + 8 + 13$.
